The '''Rebel Alliance Starfighter Corps''' was the [[Starfighter Corps]] of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. A unit made of [[Rebel pilot]]s, they participated in many major battles such as the [[Battle of Yavin]], the [[Battle of Hoth]] and the [[Battle of Endor]] during the [[Galactic Civil War]]. It was one of the Alliance's greatest assets.

==Description==
[[File:Squadrons-Xwing.png|thumb|left|250px|Notable amongst the Rebel Starfighter Corps was the T-65B X-wing, a single-person craft equipped with an astromech droid socket.]]
Although a small, unorganized resistance against Imperial rule of [[the galaxy]] had existed since at least [[19 BBY]], the Starfighter Corps itself was not organized until [[2 BBY]].<ref name="Date Note" /> In time, the Starfighter Corps would come to encompass ships including the [[BTL-A4 Y-wing assault starfighter/bomber]]s, [[T-65B X-wing starfighter]]s, [[T-65C-A2 X-wing starfighter|T-65C-A2 X-wings]], and [[UT-60D U-wing starfighter/support craft]]s.<ref name="Rogue One">''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story]]''</ref> The Alliance made use of [[scatter protocol]]s for its pilots in order to evade any Imperial efforts to locate the whole of a rebel force by tracking a single starfighter.<ref name="WeaponJedi">''[[The Weapon of a Jedi: A Luke Skywalker Adventure]]''</ref>

==History==
The Rebel Alliance Starfighter Corps was formed in [[2 BBY]] as part of the larger Rebel Alliance and its [[Alliance Military|allied military forces]].<ref name="Date Note" /> Circa 2 BBY to [[1 BBY]],<ref name="Timelines">''[[Star Wars: Timelines]]''</ref> Rebel fighters and larger craft waged a [[battle above Cianap]] against Imperial forces.<ref name="Dark Visions 1">[[Vader - Dark Visions 1|''Vader - Dark Visions'' 1]]</ref> In 1 BBY,<ref name="Timelines" /> the Rebel Alliance Starfighter Corps partook in the failed [[Attack on Lothal]],<ref name="Rebel Assault">{{Rebels|Rebel Assault}}</ref> the successful [[Liberation of Erso]],<ref name="Battlefront VR">''[[Star Wars Battlefront]]'': Rogue One: X-wing VR Mission''</ref> and the controversial [[mission to Eadu]], which set the stage for more open warfare against the Empire. In that engagement, rebel fighters fought the [[Eadu Deployment]] and destroyed the [[Eadu Energy Conversion Laboratory]].<ref name="Rogue One" />

[[File:Mobilize_the_Squadrons_TCGcore.png|thumb|left|300px|X-wings and Y-wings launch for the Battle of Yavin]]
Shortly after the [[Battle of Scarif|theft]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station|Death Star]] [[Death Star plans|plans]] at [[Scarif]], the Empire discovered the location of the Rebels' [[Great Temple|headquarters]] on [[Yavin 4]], and deployed the massive [[battle station]] to destroy the [[moon]]. The Rebels deployed a force of 22 [[X-wing starfighter]]s and 8 Y-wings for a desperate assault on the [[space station]]'s [[reactor]], which was accessible through a [[thermal exhaust port]] located at the end of the [[meridian trench]]. Despite massive casualties and fierce Imperial resistance, a young [[pilot]] named [[Luke Skywalker]] used [[the Force]] to destroy the station with a pair of [[proton torpedo]]es. Out of the initial attacking force, only 2 X-wings and a single Y-wing survived the assault.<ref name="Episode IV" />

After the Battle of Yavin, the Starfighter Corps dramatically increased the size and diversity of its forces. The Alliance was able to provide [[Saponza]]'s [[Tatooine rebel cell|rebel cell]] on [[Tatooine]] with new [[starship]] classes including [[A/SF-01 B-wing starfighter]]s, [[E-50 Landseer]]s, [[Fang-class Protectorate fighter|Fang fighters]], [[HWK-290 light freighter]]s, [[Low Altitude Assault Transport/infantry|LAAT/i]] gunships, [[RZ-1 A-wing interceptor]]s, and [[Z-95 Headhunter]]s.<ref name="Commander" /> Within a year, the Alliance had also acquired [[T-47 airspeeder]]s, which would see action at the [[Battle of Vrogas Vas]] with Cyan Squadron.<ref name="Vader Down 2" /> After the Alliance [[Hijacking of the Harbinger|hijacked]] an [[Imperial I-class Star Destroyer|''Imperial I''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Harbinger (Imperial I-class)|''Harbinger'']], Luke Skywalker and [[Sana Starros]] used the [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter]]s for patrol.<ref name="SW23">[[Star Wars (2015) 23|''Star Wars'' (2015) 23]]</ref>

The Empire secured a major victory over the rebels in the [[Battle of Mako-Ta]], which saw ninety percent of the starfighter corps destroyed.<ref name="Star Wars 55">[[Star Wars (2015) 55|''Star Wars'' (2015) 55]]</ref> In [[3 ABY]],<ref name="Galactic Atlas">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]''</ref> the T-47s were used at the [[Battle of Hoth]]. Although mainly used for pulling cargo sleds in the area surrounding [[Echo Base]], a number of [[airspeeder]]s served as effective atmospheric assault fighters.<ref name="Episode V" /> The resourceful pilots of Rogue Squadron used their harpoons and tow cables to tangle the legs of Imperial AT-AT [[walker]]s, and then blasted them with their laser cannons in their weakened state. [[Wedge Antilles]] employed this tactic successfully. [[Thane Kyrell]] of Corona Squadron used a different approach, slicing off a walker's foot pad with the sharp wing edge of his airspeeder.<ref name="LS" />

In [[4 ABY]],<ref name="Galactic Atlas" /> the Starfighter Corps struck a decisive blow to the Empire in the pivotal [[Battle of Endor]]. Under the command of [[General]] [[Landonis Balthazar Calrissian|Lando Calrissian]], the Corps led a desperate assault on the [[DS-2 Death Star II Mobile Battle Station]]. With Calrissian at the controls of the ''[[Millennium Falcon]]'', the Rebels destroyed the new battlestation, while the redeemed [[Anakin Skywalker]] sacrificed his own life to destroy [[Darth Sidious]].<ref name="Episode VI" /> Later that year, the Alliance to Restore the Republic reorganized itself into the [[New Republic]]. As part of this wider change, the Rebel Alliance Starfighter Corps was reorganized into the [[New Republic Starfighter Corps]], which continued to see action in the Galactic Civil War up to and including the [[Battle of Jakku]].<ref name="LS" />

Thirty years after the Galactic Civil War, Wedge and [[Nien Nunb|many]] [[Norra Wexley|other]] former rebel pilots would join the [[Resistance]] to [[First Order-Resistance War|fight]] against the [[First Order]].

==Training==
Though many rebel pilots lack in formal training, they make up for it with their battle experiences and usually outperformed many imperial pilots. Though, some pilots do have some formal training from the Imperial Navy before their defection to the Rebel Alliance, such as [[Jon Vander]], [[Braylen Stramm]], [[Attico Wred]], [[Biggs Darklighter]], [[Jake Farrell]], [[Lindon Javes]], [[Derek Klivian]], [[Yrica Quell]], and [[Wedge Antilles]].<ref>{{Rebels|The Antilles Extraction}}</ref><ref>''[[Star Wars Battlefront II]]''</ref>
